name: browser-devtools-mcp-vscode
description: Playwright-powered browser automation and Node.js backend debugging for VS Code and Cursor via MCP. Bundled server, no npm setup at runtime. Navigate, execute, accessibility, content inspection, interaction, Web Vitals, stubbing, React/Figma tools, and non-blocking debugging.

commands.title: Browser DevTools MCP: Install Playwright Browsers...

browserDevtoolsMcp.enable.description: Enable or disable the Browser DevTools MCP extension

browserDevtoolsMcp.telemetry.enable.description: Allow sending anonymous install/uninstall telemetry. When false, also disables telemetry for the uninstall hook.

browserDevtoolsMcp.browser.headless.description: Run browser in headless mode (no visible window)

browserDevtoolsMcp.browser.persistent.description: Enable persistent browser context (keeps cookies, localStorage, etc.)

browserDevtoolsMcp.browser.userDataDir.description: Directory for persistent browser user data

browserDevtoolsMcp.browser.useSystemBrow.description: Use system-installed browser instead of bundled Playwright browser

browserDevtoolsMcp.browser.cdp.enable.description: Enable Chrome DevTools Protocol (CDP) attach mode (Chromium only)

browserDevtoolsMcp.browser.cdp.endpointU.description: CDP endpoint URL to attach (http://host:port or ws://...)

browserDevtoolsMcp.browser.cdp.openInspe.description: When loopback CDP attach fails and Chrome is running, open chrome://inspect/#remote-debugging

browserDevtoolsMcp.browser.consoleMessag.description: Max console messages to buffer in browser (default: 1000)

browserDevtoolsMcp.browser.httpRequestsB.description: Max HTTP requests to buffer in browser (default: 1000)

browserDevtoolsMcp.install.chromium.description: Download Chromium, headless shell, and ffmpeg (Playwright) into the default cache when the extension activates.

browserDevtoolsMcp.install.firefox.description: Download Firefox (Playwright) into the default cache on activate.

browserDevtoolsMcp.install.webkit.description: Download WebKit (Playwright) into the default cache on activate.

browserDevtoolsMcp.platform.description: MCP platform: browser for web automation, node for Node.js process debugging

browserDevtoolsMcp.node.inspectorHost.description: Inspector host for Node platform when MCP runs in Docker (e.g., host.docker.internal)

browserDevtoolsMcp.node.consoleMessagesB.description: Max console messages to buffer in Node process (default: 1000)

browserDevtoolsMcp.opentelemetry.instrum.description: Comma-separated user interaction events to instrument (default: click). E.g. click,submit

browserDevtoolsMcp.bedrock.enable.description: Enable Amazon Bedrock for AI-powered features

browserDevtoolsMcp.bedrock.imageModelId.description: Amazon Bedrock model ID for image embeddings

browserDevtoolsMcp.bedrock.textModelId.description: Amazon Bedrock model ID for text embeddings

browserDevtoolsMcp.bedrock.visionModelId.description: Amazon Bedrock model ID for vision tasks

browserDevtoolsMcp.figma.accessToken.description: Figma API access token for design comparison features

browserDevtoolsMcp.toolOutputSchemaDisab.description: Omit tool output schema from MCP registration (can reduce token usage)

browserDevtoolsMcp.availableToolDomains.description: Comma-separated tool domains to enable (e.g. navigation,interaction,a11y). Empty = all. Browser: a11y, content, debug, figma, interaction, navigation, o11y, react, run, stub, sync. Node: debug, run.

browserDevtoolsMcp.showVisualizer.description: Show Visualizer: Enabled/Disabled. Controls whether the visualizer UI and its WebSocket server are active.

browserDevtoolsMcp.visualizer.wsPort.description: WebSocket port for the visualizer event stream.

scripts.vscode:prepublish: npm run build:visualizer && npm run compile
scripts.build:visualizer: cd visualizer-ui && npm install && npm run build
scripts.build: npm run build:visualizer && npm run compile
